assertions.title: GPU auto-routing activates for large corpus

steps.action: Run tg.exe search without --gpu-device-ids on 100MB file
Run tg.exe search without --gpu-device-ids on 10MB file

assertions.title: GPU falls back to CPU when no CUDA available

steps.action: Set CUDA_VISIBLE_DEVICES='99' to simulate no CUDA and run tg.exe search --gpu-device-ids 0
steps.expected: Falls back to CPU or exits with clear error
steps.observed: Exited with clear error message (CUDA initialization failed)

assertions.title: GPU falls back on init failure

steps.action: Trigger CUDA init failure using invalid CUDA_VISIBLE_DEVICES
steps.expected: Non-zero exit, clear error message. No panic.
steps.observed: Exited with code 1, error message 'CUDA initialization failed: the CUDA runtime reported an initialization error', no panic.

steps.expected: Batched copy of file data, not per-line.
steps.observed: Code uses build_batched_file_buffer and clone_htod. Verbose shows single transfer for 100MB buffer.

steps.action: Run tg.exe search with --json on 100MB file
steps.expected: JSON includes routing_backend='gpu_native' and non-empty routing_reason

frictions.description: Python CLI via `uv run` failed with an unhandled exception stack trace when explicit GPU was requested but CuDF/Torch weren't available, rather than delegating to native Rust CUDA backend. Had to use compiled Rust binary directly.
frictions.resolution: Used compiled Rust binary `rust_core/target/release/tg.exe` which worked flawlessly.

frictions.description: Cargo was not available in path, despite instructions mentioning `cargo build`.

summary: Tested 5 assertions: 5 passed. Used compiled Rust binary tg.exe instead of python uv run due to Python wrapper throwing configuration errors and not routing correctly to the native backend.
